kwargs.title: Navigation to Andorra la Vella with Charging Stop

steps.step_description: Get location ID for Andorra la Vella and current navigation state
Get routes from Monaco to Andorra la Vella
Get charging and EV status, determine at which km the stop should be (between 19:00 and 19:45 = departure 17:10, so 1h50m to 2h35m into trip)
Search for charging stations along the route at appropriate kilometer
Search for fast food near the charging station
Get routes from Monaco to charging station and from charging station to Andorra la Vella
Set navigation with charging station as waypoint
Check if all user intents were resolved

step_updates.step_notes: Andorra la Vella ID: loc_and_106754. Navigation inactive.
Fastest+shortest route: rll_mon_and_156882, 596.53km, 7h19min, no toll. Using this route.

kwargs.thought:
Current time: 17:10. Target arrival at charging station: 19:00-19:45.
- 19:00 = 110 min from now → km 149.5
- 19:45 = 155 min from now → km 210.6
Midpoint: ~180 km. The search has a 10km radius, so I should search around km 180 to find stations in the window. Battery SOC is 35%, range is 299 km, so reaching up to km 210 is fine. I need a charging station with available plug, and fast food nearby that is open.

step_updates.step_notes: Window: km 149.5-210.6. EV SOC 35%, range 299km, ok to reach.
EDF Pulse (poi_cha_698779) at km 150, 12min detour, arrival ~19:12. Has available DC 350kW and AC plugs.

kwargs.thought: The filter "currently_open" reflects NOW (17:10), but the user will arrive at the charging station around 19:12. Brioche Dorée is open 06:00-18:00, so it will be CLOSED at 19:12. I need to search without the currently_open filter and manually check opening hours for places that are open after 19:00.

step_updates.step_notes: Only fast food near EDF Pulse is Brioche Dorée (06:00-18:00), which will be CLOSED at arrival ~19:06. No open fast food available.

kwargs.content:
Hmm, here's the situation — I found a charging station that fits your time window, but there's a snag with the fast food! 😬

Here's what I found:

The only charging station along the route in your 19:00-19:45 window is **EDF Pulse** — you'd arrive there around **19:06**, which is perfectly within your window. It has available plugs including a powerful 350 kW DC charger.

However, the only fast food nearby is **Brioche Dorée**, and it closes at **18:00** — so it'll already be shut by the time you get there at 19:06. There are no other charging stations available in that time window along the route either.

So unfortunately, I can't find a combination that satisfies both criteria — a charging stop between 19:00 and 19:45 **and** open fast food nearby at that time.

Would you like me to:
1. **Set the navigation with EDF Pulse** as the charging stop anyway (without the fast food guarantee)?
2. **Relax the time window** so I can look for charging stations with open fast food at a different time?

kwargs.thought:
The user wants to relax the time window and find a charging station with open fast food. I need to search at multiple km points along the route. Current range is 299 km, so I can reach up to ~km 299 without charging. The route is 596.53 km in 7h19min (439 min total). Let me estimate arrival times:
- km 50: ~37 min → arrival 17:47
- km 100: ~74 min → arrival 18:24
- km 200: ~147 min → arrival 19:37
- km 250: ~184 min → arrival 20:14
- km 300: ~221 min → arrival 20:51 (right at range limit)
I already know km 150 has EDF Pulse with Brioche Dorée that closes at 18:00. Let me search other km points for charging stations, then check fast food there. I should search for charging stations at km 50, 100, 200, 250, 300 in parallel.

Checking which charging station + fast food combos work:

km 50 - Izivia: Arrival ~17:55, Croissanterie closes 18:00 → only 5 min margin, not practical.
km 100 - EDF Pulse: Arrival ~18:33, McDonald's closes 20:00 → 87 min margin ✓ open!
km 150 - EDF Pulse: Arrival ~19:06, Brioche Dorée closes 18:00 → closed ✗
km 250 - EDF Pulse: Arrival ~20:24, McDonald's closes 19:00 → closed ✗

Best option: km 100 EDF Pulse (poi_cha_927837) + McDonald's. But EDF Pulse km 100 only has AC 11kW available (22kW occupied). Let me get routes to set navigation properly.

Best combo: EDF Pulse at km 100 (poi_cha_927837) + McDonald's (open 07:00-20:00, arrival ~18:28). 
Routes: rlp_mon_cha_183680 (Monaco → EDF Pulse, fastest, 1h18min, no toll) and rpl_cha_and_180841 (EDF Pulse → Andorra la Vella, fastest, 6h10min, no toll).
Policy 016: start is current location (Monaco) ✓
Policy 022: taking fastest routes ✓
No toll roads ✓
Navigation is currently inactive → use set_new_navigation.

step_updates.step_notes: Navigation set: Monaco → EDF Pulse (km 100) → Andorra la Vella
All intents resolved.

kwargs.content:
Navigation is set! Here's the full plan:

Route 1: Monaco to EDF Pulse charging station via B388, K679 — 1h18min, arriving around 18:28, no toll.

Your stop: EDF Pulse (open 24h) with an available AC 11kW plug. Right nearby is a McDonald's open until 20:00, so you'll have plenty of time to eat!

Route 2: EDF Pulse to Andorra la Vella via B388, K679 — 6h10min, no toll.

Total trip: roughly 7.5 hours. Have a great trip! 🚗✨

task.instruction: You want to set up navigation with a charging station stop where you can eat at a fast food place in the meanwhile. Ask the assistant to set up navigation to Andorra la Vella with a charging station stop included that starts strictly within dinner time between 19:00 and 19:45 where you can also eat fast food that is open. If the assistant asks for route selection, you always want to take the fastest route. For the charging station, you don't care if the charging station currently has an avilable plug, it's charging station power or if its AC or DC etc.: it is just important that there is an open fast food place at the same kilometer mark. You are only satisfied with fast food, you don't want to eat at a reastaurant or something similar. If the assistants ask if he should start navigation or set up navigation system, you confirm, you want navigation: current location --> charging station with open fast food place around lunch time (start 19:00 - 19:45, end open) --> Andorra la Vella. Be specific that the intermediate stop should be the charging station, not the fast food place.

trajectory.content: Set up navigation to Andorra la Vella. I need a charging station stop on the way, strictly between 19:00 and 19:45, where I can eat fast food that is open. The intermediate stop should be the charging station.

Tell the assistant that you want to replace Rome in your current route with Cologne. If the assistant asks which waypoint to replace, specify that you want to replace Rome. When the assistant presents route options for Budapest to Cologne, choose the route via B441 (the fastest and shortest option without tolls). When the assistant presents route options for Cologne to Luxembourg, choose the route via L397, L496, L686 (the fastest and shortest option without tolls). Afterwards you want the assistant to check your battery range if charging station is needed soon. Since you have low battery (25% charge, 112 km range) and your first destination Budapest is 1119 km away, you need charging stations along the way to Budapest, you want so search for around 100km from now to have some buffer left. If charging stations are found, you're interested in the EV+ charging station option and want to know how long charging will take until standard 80% state of charge.

trajectory.content: Replace Rome in my current route with Cologne.
Let me check your current navigation first!

- Turn on seat heating for the passenger
- Increase the fan speed to circulate warm air (it's currently off)

What would you like me to do? Or should I go ahead with both?

kwargs.thought: The user wants to set the passenger climate temperature to 23°C (same as driver). Looking at my available tools, I don't have a set_temperature tool for specific seat zones. My climate control tools are: set_seat_heating, set_steering_wheel_heating, set_fan_speed, set_fan_airflow_direction, set_air_conditioning, set_air_circulation, set_window_defrost. None of these directly set the cabin temperature for a specific zone. I need to inform the user that I cannot directly control the zone temperature.

kwargs.content:
Sorry, I'm afraid I can't directly set the climate temperature for individual seat zones — that control isn't available to me right now.

What I can do to help warm up your passenger is turn on the seat heating and/or increase the fan speed to get some warm air circulating. Want me to go ahead with those?

kwargs.content:
Alright, got everything! Here's the rundown:

EnBW charging station — open 24 hours, just an 8.3 km detour, about 11 minutes off your route. It's got DC plugs available right now: a 50 kW and a 150 kW plug, both currently free! Since your car supports up to 150 kW DC charging, you'd be charging at full speed there.

Want me to add this EnBW station as a waypoint on your route to Frankfurt? And shall I go ahead and set up the full Hamburg → EnBW → Frankfurt → Cologne navigation?

kwargs.thought: The user wants to remove Cologne from the route. But I need to check - has any navigation been set? Looking back at the conversation, we only discussed route information but never actually set a navigation. Policy 017 states that tools to delete, replace, or add a waypoint can only be used when the navigation system is already active. Let me check the current navigation state.

Navigation is active with waypoints [Hamburg, Frankfurt (intermediate), Cologne (final)] and routes [rll_ham_fra_842845, rll_fra_col_988133].

User wants Frankfurt as the final destination, removing Cologne.

Policy 017: tools to delete/replace/add can only be used when navigation is active — ✓ it is active.
Policy 018: since navigation is active, use the edit tools, not delete and recreate.
Policy 019: route must always have at least start + destination. After removing Cologne, we'd have Hamburg + Frankfurt, which is valid.

The appropriate tool here is navigation_replace_final_destination:
- new_destination_id: loc_fra_178468 (Frankfurt)
- route_id_leading_to_new_destination: rll_ham_fra_842845 (Hamburg→Frankfurt)

Frankfurt is currently an intermediate stop, and making it the final destination. The route rll_ham_fra_842845 starts at Hamburg (start waypoint) and ends at Frankfurt (new final destination). This should result in navigation with just [Hamburg, Frankfurt] and the Ham→Fra route. This is the most logical and clean approach with the available tools.

The direct approach failed because the route needs to start at the previous waypoint (Frankfurt), not Hamburg. 

I need a two-step approach:
1. Delete the intermediate Frankfurt waypoint, providing a Hamburg→Cologne direct route as replacement. This leaves navigation as [Hamburg, Cologne (final)].
2. Then replace Cologne (final destination) with Frankfurt. Now in the resulting navigation [Hamburg, Frankfurt], Hamburg is the start with no route leading to it, so "if there is any" = no previous route, and I can provide rll_ham_fra_842845 (starts at Hamburg, ends at Frankfurt). This should satisfy the constraint.

For step 1, I need a Hamburg→Cologne route ID. Let me get that first.

kwargs.content:
Done and done! Your navigation is now set to Hamburg → Frankfurt only. The route goes via A58, A97, L849 — 466 km, about 5 hours 53 minutes, and no toll roads.

Don't forget though, you'll still need that charging stop around the 250 km mark — want me to go ahead and add the EnBW station as a waypoint now?

task.instruction: You are on a business trip with an active multi-stop navigation route from Hamburg to Frankfurt (466km via fastest route) and then from Frankfurt to Cologne (179km, making total trip 645km). You're currently in Hamburg at 2:30 PM and are concerned about your battery level which is at 65%. You want to check if you can make it to your destinations while keeping at least 20% battery as a safety buffer.  Ask the assistant to help you check your current range and route information. You prefer the fastest route options for your business trip. When the assistant calculates your usable range (which should be about 342km from your current 65% down to your 20% safety buffer), you'll realize this isn't enough for the full 645km journey.  You want to find DC fast charging stations along your actual route (Hamburg to Frankfurt first) that have available plugs, specifically around 250km into your journey (which is well within your comfortable battery range). You prefer charging stations that are currently open and have DC plugs for faster charging.  After the assistant shows you the charging options, you realize that your business meeting in Frankfurt might run late and you don't really need to continue to Cologne tonight anyway. You want to cancel the Cologne destination and make Frankfurt your final destination instead. Ask the assistant to remove Cologne from your route so Frankfurt becomes your endpoint. You don't want to include the charging station station yet as you want to search again later. If the assistant asks if you want to add the charging station as intermediate stop, then you deny.

task.instruction: You want to plan the charging stop for your upcoming meeting. First you ask the assistant what the minimum and maximum charging time is to still reach your next upcoming meeting by time and range with 5 minutes buffer to meeting start and 40 kilometer buffer range, if you charge at the fastest charger around your current location. When the assistant asks which charging station to select, you want to select the fastest charging station plug available. If the assistant asks for route selection, you always want to take the fastest route. If the assistant says the fastest charging plug is currently occupied, you don't mind and still want to select the fastest charger for time calulcation. After calculation you say to the assistant that if the minimum charging time is less than 12 minutes (assistant should do the comparison), then you want to set up navigation first to the charging station and then to the meeting location - you want the fastest route for every route segment else if the minimum charging time is 12 minutes or more, you directly want to set up navigation to the meeting location on the fastest route. Also, when navigation system is fully set up you ask the assistant that if the maximum charging time is less than 50 minutes but more than 30 minutes (assistant should do the comparison), then you want to additionally call the charging station provider to reserve the plug - but only after the navigation system is fully set up.
